# KulmanLab

> Free, browser-based 2D CAD editor. No installation, no account, no data uploaded — your files stay on your machine.

## Links

- App: https://app.kulmanlab.com
- Landing page: https://kulmanlab.com
- Docs: https://kulmanlab.com/docs
- Changelog: https://kulmanlab.com/changelog
- FAQ: https://kulmanlab.com/faq
- GitHub: https://github.com/volodymyr4509/kulmanlab
- Reddit: https://www.reddit.com/r/kulmanlab/
- Full reference (llms-full.txt): https://kulmanlab.com/llms-full.txt

## German (Deutsch)

- Landing page (DE): https://kulmanlab.com/de/
- Docs (DE): https://kulmanlab.com/de/docs
- Changelog (DE): https://kulmanlab.com/de/changelog
- FAQ (DE): https://kulmanlab.com/de/faq

## Description

KulmanLab is a professional-grade 2D CAD editor that runs entirely in the browser. There is nothing to install — open the URL and start drawing. All geometry is stored in your browser's localStorage; no file is ever sent to a server. The app works offline after the first load.

It is designed for engineers, architects, and technical illustrators who need a capable DXF-compatible tool without the friction of desktop software or the privacy concerns of cloud CAD platforms.

## Key features

- Full command terminal with history, autocomplete, and keyboard navigation (like AutoCAD)
- 50+ drawing commands: Line, Polyline, Circle, Arc, Ellipse, Rectangle, Spline, Hatch, Text (MTEXT), Dimensions, Leaders, and more
- Layer management with color, linetype, and lineweight per layer
- Grip editing on all entity types (lines, arcs, polylines, dimensions, text, viewports)
- Snapping: endpoint, midpoint, center, intersection, perpendicular, tangent, nearest, grid
- Angle tracking with configurable increments (10°, 15°, 20°, 30°, 45°, 90°)
- Layout / paper space with multiple layouts and viewport editing
- ISO 5455 scale selector for viewports
- Print manager: PDF and PNG/WebP export with DPI control
- DXF import and export (compatible with LibreCAD, QCAD, BricsCAD, FreeCAD)
- Match Properties command (copy color, layer, linetype, lineweight)
- Properties panel showing entity details
- Undo/redo with full history
- Adaptive CAD grid with snap-to-grid
- Themes: dark mode by default, teal (#4A8580) accent

## Workflow

1. **Model** — draw geometry in model space using commands, snapping, and layers
2. **Configure paper format** — switch to a layout tab, set the paper size, and add viewports at the desired scale
3. **Print / export** — open the print manager (Ctrl+P) to export PDF or PNG, or use File → Export DXF

## Frequently asked questions

**Is KulmanLab really free?**
Yes — completely free. No subscription, no credit card, no hidden plan. The full feature set is available to everyone with no limits.

**Do I need to create an account?**
No. There is no login, no sign-up, and no email required. Open the app and start drawing immediately.

**What browsers are supported?**
Any modern desktop browser — Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge all work. KulmanLab is desktop-only; mobile and tablet are not currently supported.

**Does it work offline?**
Yes. After the first load the app is cached and runs entirely without an internet connection. Your drawings are stored locally so nothing requires a server.

**Where are my drawings stored?**
In your browser's localStorage — on your own device. Nothing is ever uploaded to a server. Each browser on each device has its own independent storage.

**What happens if I clear my browser data?**
Your saved drawings will be lost. Always export a DXF copy of anything important before clearing browser storage.

**Can I open .dwg files?**
KulmanLab supports DXF (Drawing Exchange Format), not the proprietary DWG format. Most desktop CAD applications can export DXF, so export from your source app first.

**Which export formats are available?**
DXF for round-tripping with other CAD tools, and PDF or PNG/WebP for print-ready output. The print manager lets you set DPI and paper size before exporting.

**Does it have a command terminal?**
Yes. Type commands directly — LINE, CIRCLE, MOVE, TRIM, and 50+ others. The terminal supports history (arrow keys), autocomplete (Tab), and exact numeric input.

**Is there layer support?**
Full layer management: create, rename, freeze, lock, and isolate layers. Set color, linetype, and lineweight per layer. All layer data is preserved in DXF export.

**What dimension types are available?**
Linear, aligned, radius, diameter, angular, and continue dimensions. All stored as standard DXF DIMENSION entities compatible with other DXF-capable CAD tools.

**Can I edit geometry with grips?**
Yes. Select any entity to reveal grip handles at key points — endpoints, midpoints, centers, arc ends. Drag to reshape directly on the canvas, or type a value while dragging for exact placement.

**Is real-time collaboration supported?**
Not currently. KulmanLab is a single-user tool and drawings stay local. Cloud sync and collaboration are outside the current scope.

**Are KulmanLab DXF files compatible with other CAD software?**
Yes. KulmanLab reads and writes AC1032 DXF — a widely supported version. Files open correctly in LibreCAD, QCAD, BricsCAD, FreeCAD, and any other DXF-capable tool.

**What is the difference between DXF and DWG?**
DXF is an open, text-based exchange format designed for interoperability. DWG is a proprietary binary format. KulmanLab uses DXF, which is readable by virtually every CAD tool.

**Can KulmanLab replace my desktop CAD software?**
For 2D drafting — yes. KulmanLab has a full command terminal, layers, snapping, dimensions, grip editing, and DXF compatibility. It does not support 3D, blocks/xrefs, or parametric constraints.

**Does KulmanLab collect my drawing data?**
No. Your drawings stay in your browser and are never sent anywhere. KulmanLab uses Google Analytics for anonymous usage metrics (page views, session counts) — no drawing content is involved.

**Are cookies used?**
Only the cookies set automatically by Google Analytics. No tracking beyond that, no advertising cookies, no third-party data sharing.

## Recent releases

**June 2026 — New website, landing page, docs & FAQ**
Brand-new landing page at kulmanlab.com with screenshot carousel, feature grid, comparison table, and workflow overview. Command reference docs at kulmanlab.com/docs. FAQ page. This changelog. llms.txt and llms-full.txt for AI assistants.

**June 2026 — Text editor polish & web fonts**
Text mirror adjusts rotation and attachment point for readability. Web fonts in the text editor font picker. Cursor height matches text size. Strikethrough in text toolbar. Per-row line height and per-character text height/font (MTEXT). MTEXT frame sizing and live resize grip. Text editing creates a single undo entry on close.

**June 2026 — On-canvas text editing**
Edit text directly on the canvas with a blinking caret. Text selection with Shift+Arrow, click, click-drag. I-beam cursor while text editor is active. Bold/Italic toolbar with cursor-aware active state. MTEXT wrap frame with four resize grips.

**June 2026 — Angle tracking, snap-to-grid & MTEXT**
Configurable angle tracking (10°–90°) with arc indicator. Snap-to-grid toggle with dot grid at intersections. Grid dots on layout paper space. Custom scale input for viewport dropdown. Multi-line MTEXT parsing and rendering. Adaptive coordinate display precision.

## Comparison with alternatives

| Feature | KulmanLab | LibreCAD | QCAD | BricsCAD | AutoCAD | Onshape | AutoCAD Web |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Runs in browser | Yes | No | No | No | No | Yes | Yes |
| Free | Yes | Yes | Community | No | No | Free plan | No |
| No account required | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | No | No | No |
| Works offline | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | 30d check | No | No |
| Files stay local | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | No |
| Command terminal | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | No | Yes |
| Layout / paper space | Yes | No | No | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| DXF import & export | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |

## Who it is for

- Engineers and designers who want CAD-style commands without the subscription
- Users on locked-down machines where installing desktop software is not allowed
- Privacy-conscious users who do not want drawings stored on third-party servers
- Students and hobbyists who need a capable free CAD tool
- CNC and laser cutting workflows that need DXF files prepared or tweaked
- Teams evaluating browser-based CAD for internal tooling

## Privacy

KulmanLab collects no drawing data. Your DXF files and canvas state are stored locally in localStorage — never uploaded. Google Analytics is used for anonymous usage metrics (page views, session counts). The site sets cookies only for GA. No account, no login, no server-side storage.

## Release cadence

KulmanLab ships updates continuously — typically every few days. See https://kulmanlab.com/changelog for the full release history.
